article.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, the First Open Model to Unify Vision, Audio, and Language in a Single Efficient Architecture
article.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Nano Omni combines text, image, video, and audio processing in one 30B-parameter open model that activates only 3B parameters per pass, delivering up to 9x the throughput of comparable open omni models.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano Omni on April 28, 2026, an open multimodal model that processes text, images, video, and audio through a single unified architecture, according to the [NVIDIA blog](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-ai-agents/). The model carries 30 billion total parameters but activates only 3 billion per forward pass through a mixture-of-experts design, and NVIDIA says it delivers up to 9x higher throughput than other open omni models at comparable interactivity levels. Weights are released under the NVIDIA Nemotron Open Model License.

## Architecture

Nemotron 3 Nano Omni is built on what NVIDIA calls a hybrid Mamba-Transformer backbone. According to the [NVIDIA Developer Blog](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-nemotron-3-nano-omni-powers-multimodal-agent-reasoning-in-a-single-efficient-open-model/), the design combines Mamba layers "for sequence and memory efficiency with transformer layers for precise reasoning." The [technical report on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-intelligence) details the layer composition: 23 Mamba selective state-space layers, 23 Mixture-of-Experts layers with 128 experts, top-6 routing, and a shared expert, plus 6 grouped-query attention layers.

The model handles visual input through the C-RADIOv4-H vision encoder, which supports dynamic resolution from 1,024 to 13,312 visual patches per image — equivalent to 512×512 to 1840×1840 for square images — enabling native aspect ratio processing, according to [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-intelligence). Audio is routed through the Parakeet-TDT-0.6B-v2 encoder, which was trained on inputs up to 1,200 seconds, with the model's context window supporting more than five hours of audio.

Video frames are compressed through a Conv3D tubelet embedding that fuses consecutive frame pairs, halving the number of vision tokens. An inference-time feature called Efficient Video Sampling (EVS) further reduces token counts. As described on the [Hugging Face blog](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-intelligence): "The first frame of the video is kept entirely, then for each subsequent frame, EVS keeps the 'dynamic' tokens where the video is changing and drops the 'static' ones where nothing has changed from the previous frame."

The overall context window extends to 256,000 tokens, according to the [NVIDIA blog](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-ai-agents/).

## Training

The model was pretrained on 25 trillion tokens, per [The Next Web](https://thenextweb.com/news/nvidia-nemotron-nano-omni-multimodal-agent-edge). The [NVIDIA Developer Blog](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-nemotron-3-nano-omni-powers-multimodal-agent-reasoning-in-a-single-efficient-open-model/) reports that adapter and encoder training involved approximately 127 billion tokens across mixed modalities, followed by approximately 124 million curated multimodal post-training samples. NVIDIA generated approximately 11.4 million synthetic visual question-answering pairs totaling around 45 billion tokens using its NeMo Data Designer tooling from real-world PDFs, which the [Hugging Face technical post](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-intelligence) says yielded a 2.19x improvement in overall accuracy on the MMLongBench-Doc document comprehension benchmark.

Reinforcement learning training involved more than 2.3 million environment rollouts across 25 configurations, according to the [NVIDIA Developer Blog](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-nemotron-3-nano-omni-powers-multimodal-agent-reasoning-in-a-single-efficient-open-model/). The [arXiv technical report](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24954) describes a "multi-stage training strategy that progressively introduces new modalities and scales context length."

## Benchmark Results

Benchmark scores from the [Hugging Face release post](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-intelligence) show Nemotron 3 Nano Omni outperforming its predecessor and Qwen3-Omni 30B-A3B across most evaluated tasks:

- **Document comprehension** (MMLongBench-Doc): 57.5, compared to 38.0 for the predecessor Nemotron Nano V2 VL and 49.5 for Qwen3-Omni 30B-A3B
- **Chart understanding** (CharXiv reasoning): 63.6, compared to 41.3 and 61.1
- **OCR** (OCRBenchV2-En): 65.8, compared to 61.2
- **OS interaction** (OSWorld): 47.4, compared to 11.0 and 29.0
- **Video QA** (Video-MME): 72.2, compared to 63.0 and 70.5
- **Video and audio comprehension** (WorldSense): 55.4, compared to 54.0 for Qwen3-Omni
- **Voice interaction** (VoiceBench): 89.4, compared to 88.8 for Qwen3-Omni
- **Automatic speech recognition** (HF Open ASR, lower is better): 5.95, compared to 6.55 for Qwen3-Omni

The [NVIDIA Developer Blog](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-nemotron-3-nano-omni-powers-multimodal-agent-reasoning-in-a-single-efficient-open-model/) reports up to 9.2x greater effective system capacity for video reasoning and up to 7.4x greater effective system capacity for multi-document tasks compared to alternative models.

## Adoption

Several organizations have already integrated the model into products. Gautier Cloix, CEO of H Company, said: "To build useful agents, you can't wait seconds for a model to interpret a screen. By building on Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, our agents can rapidly interpret full HD screen recordings — something that wasn't practical before." The quote appears on the [NVIDIA blog](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-ai-agents/).

Other early adopters listed by NVIDIA include Aible, Applied Scientific Intelligence, Eka Care, Foxconn, Palantir, and Pyler, while Dell Technologies, DocuSign, Infosys, Oracle, and Zefr are listed as evaluating the model, according to the [NVIDIA blog](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-ai-agents/).

The [NVIDIA blog](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-ai-agents/) also notes that the broader Nemotron model family has reached 50 million downloads over the past year.

## Availability

Model weights are available in BF16, FP8, and NVFP4 precision on Hugging Face, as well as through OpenRouter, build.nvidia.com, and more than 25 partner platforms, according to the [NVIDIA Developer Blog](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-nemotron-3-nano-omni-powers-multimodal-agent-reasoning-in-a-single-efficient-open-model/). The model supports inference through vLLM and NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM on Ampere, Hopper, and Blackwell GPU families.

## What We Don't Know

NVIDIA has not independently published detailed comparisons against closed-weight frontier models such as GPT-5.5 or Claude Opus 4.7. The OSWorld and ScreenSpot-Pro benchmarks measure computer use in constrained research settings, and real-world performance on enterprise software stacks remains unverified by third parties. The model's performance on tasks requiring simultaneous audio and video with long-form text instructions has not been reported beyond the curated benchmark suite.
